label: Hantavirus-associated cluster on a Dutch-flag cruise ship (2026)
deck: Facts below follow the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control preliminary assessment of 6 May 2026. The incident is described as rapidly evolving.

primary_source.name: ECDC — Hantavirus-associated cluster of illness on a cruise ship

secondary_source.name: WHO — Response to hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship

situation_snapshot.notification: ECDC notified 2 May 2026 by the Netherlands through the EU Early Warning and Response System (EWRS).
situation_snapshot.setting: Cruise ship in the South Atlantic; Dutch flag; ~149 people on board from 23 nationalities (including passengers and crew from multiple EU/EEA countries).
situation_snapshot.counts_as_of_6_may: Seven cases in the hantavirus-associated cluster: three deaths; one person medically evacuated to South Africa and in intensive care; two symptomatic on board; one person diagnosed after disembarking and returning to Switzerland.
situation_snapshot.laboratory: Samples from two patients PCR-positive for hantavirus; one additional patient PCR-positive for Andes virus (ANDV). Further laboratory work ongoing.
situation_snapshot.working_hypothesis: Some travellers may have been exposed to ANDV in Argentina before embarkation (endemic area), with subsequent transmission to others on board during close, prolonged contact in a closed setting.
situation_snapshot.risk_eu_population: ECDC: risk to the general EU/EEA population from spread off the ship is assessed as very low when infection prevention and control measures are applied; ANDV rodent reservoir is not expected to establish in Europe.

sankey.split_rule: Precautionary contact pool vs. persons in the seven-case hantavirus cluster (ECDC 6 May)
sankey.outcome_rule: Cluster of seven: severe HPS-compatible illness; PCR hantavirus (n=2) and ANDV (n=1) among confirmations to date
sankey.prediction_note: Scenario bands are illustrative only: they blend observed counts with simple branching intuition (post-control R≈0.96 vs. delayed isolation). They are not ECDC or WHO forecasts.

scenarios.label: Base (incubation tail)
scenarios.rule: Some contacts still inside 7–42 d window

decision_tree.if: Unexplained cluster of severe febrile illness with respiratory and/or gastrointestinal features on a passenger vessel, especially after time ashore in South America.
decision_tree.then: Treat as a public health event: activate ship medical surge, line list, exposure history (ports, excursions, cabin contacts), and notify competent authorities per flag state, port state, and international health regulations as applicable.
decision_tree.note: Matches the initial signal ECDC describes from the Dutch notification.

decision_tree.if: Any person develops rapid progression to pneumonia, acute respiratory distress, or shock.
decision_tree.then: Arrange urgent medical evacuation to a facility that can provide intensive supportive care; obtain diagnostic samples before or during transfer.
decision_tree.note: ECDC reports at least four of seven cases progressed rapidly; one evacuee tested hantavirus PCR-positive on 3 May.

decision_tree.if: PCR shows hantavirus and/or ANDV, or clinical picture strongly suggests HPS in an appropriate exposure context.
decision_tree.then: Isolate confirmed and suspected cases on board where feasible; cohort nursing; trace and monitor close contacts; reinforce hand hygiene, respiratory etiquette, and distancing.
decision_tree.note: ECDC: ANDV person-to-person transmission is rare but documented with close, prolonged contact.

decision_tree.if: Early in investigation, everyone on board shares common indoor spaces and social activities (closed environment).
decision_tree.then: Apply the precautionary principle: consider all on board close contacts until epidemiology refines risk tiers.
decision_tree.note: Explicit in ECDC risk assessment for this cluster.

decision_tree.if: Symptomatic patient will disembark or be transferred between jurisdictions.
decision_tree.then: Use PCR and/or serology for diagnosis; communicate that negative tests early in illness may not exclude infection; give written follow-up and reporting instructions.
decision_tree.note: ECDC recommendations; EURL network can assist EU labs lacking ANDV capacity.

decision_tree.if: Healthcare facility receives a patient from the ship or an epidemiologically linked contact.
decision_tree.then: Apply standard and droplet precautions; escalate to airborne precautions if aerosol-generating procedures are performed.
decision_tree.note: ECDC IPC guidance for ANDV-compatible illness.

decision_tree.if: Media or port communities ask whether shore-side outbreaks are likely in Europe.
decision_tree.then: Cite reservoir ecology: ANDV is not expected to establish in European rodents; focus messaging on case/contact management rather than widespread community contagion.
decision_tree.note: ECDC stresses very low EU/EEA population risk under proper controls.
